per_lens.summary: SOUND. The strong-cov null is genuinely NO-CONCEPT and the 22/300 (auc 0.81) and 78/300 (auc 0.94) false-confirms are a real certifier weakness, not a construction artifact. Verified by recomputation: (a) w.v_hat ~1e-14 (exact orthogonality forced by v -= (v.w_hat)w_hat), so w.Zp==w.Z and predict_proba(Zp)==predict_proba(Z) to ~1e-14 -> Y* drawn session-invariantly -> P(Y|Z) session-invariant to MACHINE PRECISION. No _prior_resample, no relabel, no non-orthogonality bug; the draw uses the correct session-invariant p. (b) DECISIVE oracle-null test: regenerating Y* from the exact true boundary and recomputing T through the identical cross-fit places the observed T squarely in the bulk of the true null (oracle_p ~ Uniform, 0.025-0.99; oracle_Tz within +/-2 across all 12 cohorts), while the certifier's fixed-margin null centers ~7-10x too low (~0.0005 vs true ~0.004) with sd 2-3x too small, pinning fixed_margin_p at the floor and T_z at 10-28. Since observed T and the oracle draws are i.i.d. from the same session-invariant law, a correctly-specified test does NOT reject -- the failure is localized exactly to the certifier's margin-fixed/parametric null being mis-centered and under-dispersed under a strong covariate (the predicted fitted-null under-dispersion mode). (c) valid=True / n_invalid=0 for all 600 records (genuine confident false-confirms, not abstentions); rate rises monotonically 1.7% -> 7.3% -> 26%; ALL confirms also defeat the subject-consistency gate (studentized_p<=0.0249, LCB>0), because the systematic covariate produces a systematic per-subject interaction overfit. (d) delta=0 gives Zp==Z bit-identically (==NULL_cov DGP), session_auc median ~0.52 matching the real soft covariate 0.525-0.527 -- valid anchor. Mechanism: the shift lands 100% in the certifier's rank-3 PC interaction basis (sessmean_in_top3PC=1.000); a strong marginal covariate inflates the finite-sample cross-fit interaction-overfit T into the genuine-concept range (artifact-T up to 0.0085 vs POS_concept median 0.0097), and the observed_T>=tau gate that filtered the soft-covariate artifact (T<0.006) cannot filter it. This is a sound demonstration that the router's type-I control collapses under a strong covariate.

MULTI-BLOCK DRIFT (part A) is computationally sound and exactly reproducible, but its reach is narrower than the "generalizes across held-out blocks" framing implies. VERIFIED by recompute from raw JSONL (/home/infres/yinwang/realeeg_feas/router_stage1/multiblock/): (a) seeds follow seed=base+CIDX*1e6+r exactly on every record; the four blocks' seed ranges (90e6:9.1-9.7e6 offset region, 100/110/120e6 analogously) plus calibration 80e6 are fully DISJOINT (programmatic pairwise-intersection check = zero overlap) and RNG-independent. (b) NULL_cov allow=[5,1,5,3] reproduces exactly; per-block CP95u=[0.0347,0.0157,0.0347,0.0256], max_CP95u=0.0347 CONFIRMED; all four <=7-cap; NULL_cov_plus_label=[4,6,3,4] (max 6, CP95u max 0.0391); pooled NULL_cov=14/1200=1.17% (CP95u 1.82%). (d) POS_concept=[52,61,56,54] = [17.3,20.3,18.7,18.0]% -> stably ~17-20%, mean 18.6%. HOWEVER the drift concern is ADDRESSED only in a narrow regime, not in general: the four "blocks" are NOT independent datasets -- every cohort resamples 30-of-54 subjects from ONE fixed Lee2019 cache (10800 epochs, 54 subjects) at the SAME soft covariate (session_auc~0.521-0.523, overlap_frac 0.989 in all four), so the measured spread is Monte-Carlo subsampling noise on one dataset at one stressor, not genuine distributional or covariate drift. Part A varies only the RNG seed; the red-team's actual worry (strong covariate) is untouched here and is REFUTED the wrong way by part B (22/300, 78/300 breaches). Honest verdict: block-drift for same-data + soft-covariate is now well-characterized and rock-solid; the general drift/type-I-doubling concern is NOT-YET-REFUTED (and part B is a live counterexample). Files: /home/infres/yinwang/realeeg_feas/router_stage1/multiblock/r1_multiblock_tables.json, r1_multiblock_eval.py (/home/infres/yinwang/realeeg_feas/r1_multiblock_eval.py), realeeg_internal_forensic.py (seed/run_cohort), router_stage1_tau_lock.json.

serious_issues.summary: Blocks are RNG-independent but NOT independent samples: all four resample 30-of-54 subjects from the SAME fixed Lee2019 cache (54 subjects, 10800 epochs), so measured 'block-to-block drift' is Monte-Carlo subsampling variance on one dataset, not cross-cohort/cross-site distributional drift. The claim to 'generalize across held-out blocks' overreaches what same-pool resamples can establish.

The stressor is held constant across all four blocks (session_auc 0.520-0.523, overlap_frac 0.989 everywhere), so part A varies only the seed, not the covariate strength that the red-team flagged. Part A therefore does not touch the #1 caveat; part B (strong covariate) shows the SAME locked tau breaching the 7-cap at 22/300 (d1.5) and 78/300 (d2.5) -- the general drift concern is refuted in the adverse direction, not addressed.

all_issues.summary: Scope: the collapse is demonstrated for a covariate injected along the leading PC orthogonal to w, which lands 100% inside the certifier's rank-3 interaction basis (verified sessmean_in_top3PC=1.000). This is the generic case for a strong marginal shift (strong covariates dominate leading PCs, which is exactly what the PC-interaction basis models), but a strong covariate lying OUTSIDE the top-3 PCs is untested, so the claim is precisely 'type-I control collapses under a strong covariate occupying the certifier's interaction subspace'.

Single held-out block per strength (base 100e6, n=300 at delta 1.5 and 2.5); the multiblock drift study (A) only stress-tested the SOFT covariate, so there is no across-block variance on the strong-cov false-confirm counts themselves (though the effect size, T_z 10-28 and the 7-10x null mis-centering, dwarfs plausible block-to-block noise).

Monotonic dose-response rests on three points (auc 0.53/0.81/0.94 -> 1.7%/7.3%/26%); the curve is thin, though the direction is unambiguous and mechanistically explained by artifact-T inflating past the fixed tau.

The demonstration establishes the failure with the certifier held byte-fixed; it does not by itself show the failure is unfixable. The oracle-null evidence I computed indicates a Bernoulli/true-law or covariate-robust null would restore control here, so the weakness is specifically the fixed-margin swap null, not the cross-fit T statistic (observed T is an unbiased typical draw from its own true null). This scopes the indictment but is outside the demonstration's stated claim.

Blocks are RNG-independent but NOT independent samples: all four resample 30-of-54 subjects from the SAME fixed Lee2019 cache (54 subjects, 10800 epochs), so measured 'block-to-block drift' is Monte-Carlo subsampling variance on one dataset, not cross-cohort/cross-site distributional drift. The claim to 'generalize across held-out blocks' overreaches what same-pool resamples can establish.

The stressor is held constant across all four blocks (session_auc 0.520-0.523, overlap_frac 0.989 everywhere), so part A varies only the seed, not the covariate strength that the red-team flagged. Part A therefore does not touch the #1 caveat; part B (strong covariate) shows the SAME locked tau breaching the 7-cap at 22/300 (d1.5) and 78/300 (d2.5) -- the general drift concern is refuted in the adverse direction, not addressed.

n=4 blocks cannot bound the drift tail: raw counts span [1..5] (a 5x range), per-block CP95u reach 0.0347 with heavily overlapping intervals. The pooled 14/1200=1.17% (CP95u 1.82%) is a within-distribution type-I point estimate, not a distribution-shift bound; four correlated points give essentially no information about the tail the red-team cared about.

The router's entire type-I margin is the locked tau, calibrated on the same 54-subject pool: B3-alone false-confirms 62/300 (20.7%) under the covariate null (method_confirm_baseline in the tau lock); tau screens 62->3 on calibration (80e6) but held-out 90e6 already drifted UP to 5, indicating mild calibration optimism. Nothing in part A tests whether this same-pool artifact transfers off-distribution.

Positive controls verified: allow counts, CP95u, seed formula, and full seed disjointness (incl. vs calibration 80e6) reproduce exactly; POS utility is stable at 17.3-20.3%; all nulls within the pre-registered 7-cap; 0 invalid cohorts. The experiment is internally valid and honestly reported -- the issue is scope/interpretation, not correctness.

synthesis:
Recompute confirms the load-bearing numbers exactly: strong-cov 22/300 and 78/300 with n_invalid=0; multi-block fresh blocks NULL_cov=[1,5,3] (+90e6=[5]) all ≤7, NULL_cov_plus_label=[4,6,3,4], POS=[52,61,56,54]. The module code confirms the orthogonalization (v -= (v·ŵ)ŵ) and session-invariant draw (Y* from predict_proba(Zp) with w·Zp==w·Z). Here is the combined readout.

---

SYNTHESIS READOUT — CSC-realEEG R1 scale-up (development-diagnostic, NO tag)

Both lenses reproduced from raw JSONL; I re-verified the decision-load-bearing counts independently (router allow = b3==CONCEPT_CONFIRMED AND observed_T≥tau=0.004587196, tau LOCKED). All four strong-cov/multi-block allow counts match to the unit.

**(1) Is the strong-cov null a SOUND demonstration that type-I control COLLAPSES under a strong covariate? — YES, sound.**
The construction is a genuine ground-truth NO_CONCEPT: the shift is forced orthogonal to the pooled logistic boundary (v -= (v·ŵ)ŵ, verified w·v̂ ~1e-14), so w·Zp==w·Z and Y* is drawn from a session-invariant P(Y|Z) to machine precision — only the marginal P(Z|session) moves. The false-confirms are therefore real certifier errors, not construction artifacts. They are also genuine confident confirms, not abstentions: valid=True / n_invalid=0 for all 600 records, recomputed. The dose-response is monotone and breaches the pre-registered 7/300 (2.33%) cap: auc0.53→~1.7% (anchor, ≈real soft covariate), auc0.81→22/300=7.3%, auc0.94→78/300=26%. The mechanism is pinned by the strongcov lens's decisive oracle-null test: regenerating Y* from the true boundary through the identical cross-fit puts observed T in the bulk of the true null (oracle_p≈Uniform), while the certifier's fixed-margin/parametric swap-null is mis-centered ~7-10x too low and 2-3x under-dispersed, pinning fixed_margin_p at the floor and inflating T_z to 10.6/17.2. The shift lands 100% in the certifier's rank-3 PC interaction basis (sessmean_in_top3PC=1.000). Verdict: SOUND demonstration that the router's type-I control collapses under a strong covariate that occupies the interaction subspace.

**(2) Is the multi-block drift concern now ADDRESSED? — PARTIALLY; only within the soft-covariate regime.**
Part A is computationally sound and exactly reproducible: 3 fresh RNG-disjoint blocks (100/110/120e6) plus the original 90e6 give NULL_cov=[5,1,5,3] (max 5), NULL_cov_plus_label=[4,6,3,4] (max 6), all ≤ the 7-cap; POS utility is stable at 17.3-20.3%. BUT the four "blocks" are not independent datasets — all resample 30-of-54 subjects from ONE fixed Lee2019 cache at the SAME soft covariate (session_auc 0.520-0.523, overlap_frac 0.989 everywhere). So Part A varies only the seed, and what it establishes is Monte-Carlo subsampling stability on one dataset at one weak stressor, not distributional or covariate drift. The red-team's actual #1 worry (strong covariate) is untouched by Part A and is refuted in the adverse direction by Part B. n=4 correlated points (span [1..5], overlapping CP95u up to ~0.035) cannot bound the drift tail. Verdict: same-data soft-covariate block-drift is now well-characterized and rock-solid; the general drift / type-I concern is NOT-YET-REFUTED and has a live counterexample.

**(3) Honest OPERATING ENVELOPE.**
Robust to seed/subsample block-drift AND to weak session covariates (session_auc≈0.52): NULL false-confirm stays ≤5/300, comfortably inside the 7-cap, and POS utility holds ~18-20%. NOT robust to covariate strength: once the marginal session shift is strong enough to land in the certifier's leading-PC interaction basis (auc≥0.81), the fixed-margin null's under-dispersion is no longer masked and type-I blows past the cap (7.3% → 26%). The router is a SOFT-covariate-regime diagnostic tool, not a general type-I controller. The tau gate is the entire type-I margin, calibrated on the same 54-subject pool (B3-alone false-confirms 62/300 under the covariate null → screened to 3 on 80e6 calibration but already 5 on 90e6 held-out — mild calibration optimism), and nothing here shows that screen transfers off-distribution.

**(4) Frozen-protocol eligibility.**
NOT eligible as a general type-I controller. The strong-cov failure is a HARD boundary, not a tunable margin: the router "works" only because a weak covariate keeps the certifier's fixed-margin null under-dispersion latent (observed T stays below tau); a strong covariate inflates the finite-sample cross-fit interaction-overfit T (artifact-T up to ~0.0085) into the genuine-concept range (POS median ~0.0097), and the observed_T≥tau gate that filtered the soft-covariate artifact (T<0.006) provably cannot filter it. If ever frozen, the protocol must be scoped with an explicit precondition — a pre-deployment covariate-strength gate (e.g. session_auc/marginal-shift bound below the auc0.81 breach point) — or, per the strongcov lens's low-severity note, the fixed-margin swap null replaced by a covariate-robust/true-law (Bernoulli oracle) null that the oracle test indicates would restore control. The cross-fit T statistic itself is not indicted (observed T is a typical draw from its own true null); the fixed-margin parametric null is.

**(5) Validity issues that MUST be disclosed.**
- Both diagnostics are single-dataset (one Lee2019 54-subject cache); "generalizes across held-out blocks" overreaches — it is same-pool subsampling stability, not cross-site/cross-cohort drift.
- The strong-cov collapse is demonstrated only for a covariate along the leading PC orthogonal to w, which lands 100% in the rank-3 interaction basis; a strong covariate OUTSIDE the top-3 PCs is untested — the precise claim is "type-I collapses under a strong covariate occupying the certifier's interaction subspace."
- Strong-cov has one held-out block per strength (base 100e6, n=300); no across-block variance on the strong-cov counts (effect size T_z 10-28 dwarfs plausible block noise, but state it).
- Dose-response rests on 3 points; direction unambiguous, curve thin.
- tau is calibrated on the same pool it is evaluated near (calibration 80e6 → 3; held-out 90e6 → 5): mild calibration optimism; the same-pool artifact's off-distribution transfer is untested.

BOTTOM LINE: The scale-up strengthens the honest negative. Part A confirms the router is stable under seed/subsample block-drift at a soft covariate; Part B is a sound, reproduced demonstration that its type-I control collapses (7.3% at auc0.81, 26% at auc0.94) once the covariate is strong. Net: a soft-covariate-regime diagnostic with a hard covariate-strength boundary — development-diagnostic only, not-deployable, NO tag.
